page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-24GB is a virtual server based on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 shared vCPUs mapped to physical AMD EPYC 7542 processors running at 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 24.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware included with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate a performance profile that leans toward budget-oriented workloads. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng is rated as poor and weak respectively. Geekbench multi-core scores and software compilation benchmarks also fall into the weak category. However, the server demonstrates average performance in memory bandwidth for small blocks, Redis SET operations at 5.87 million operations per second, and single-threaded gzip compression.

The primary tradeoff of this server is its shared CPU allocation, which limits sustained high-compute performance, as evidenced by the weak multi-core and database operation benchmarks. Qualitatively, the instance offers a cost-effective option for workloads requiring a moderate memory footprint of 24.0 GB without the premium cost of dedicated CPU resources.

This configuration is suited for general-purpose applications with low-to-moderate CPU utilization, such as basic web serving, Redis caching, lightweight text processing, and small-scale LLM inference tasks using models under 1B parameters. It is less suitable for heavy database operations, software compilation, or medium-to-large language model inference.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-24GB is an x86_64 virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor, powered by 4 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. It features 24.0 GB of RAM, yielding 6.0 GB per core, with no bundled local storage or GPU. Performance benchmarks show a split profile: while single-core and multi-core CPU stress tests are weak to poor, the instance achieves average results in Redis SET operations, small-block memory bandwidth, and single-threaded gzip compression. Due to its shared CPU allocation, this server presents a cost-effective option for memory-reliant but compute-light workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose tasks, caching, and lightweight web serving, whereas intensive database operations and software compilation are limited by the shared resource model.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-24GB is a KVM-based virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 24.0 GB RAM, offering a budget-friendly option for memory-balanced, low-intensity workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-4xCPU-24GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 24.0 GB RAM for budget-friendly general workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU virtual server offering balanced memory capacity for lightweight general-purpose workloads and caching applications.

24.0 GB of system memory providing 6.0 GB of RAM per core
KVM hypervisor architecture with no bundled local storage or GPU
Average performance in Redis SET operations and small-block memory bandwidth
Cost-effective shared-resource model for budget-conscious deployments
Best suited for lightweight web serving, caching, and low-intensity general tasks
